faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1: The 5-hour window is our billing cycle unit. The system resets your request count every 5 hours. Within a window, you can use the API normally as long as your request count stays under the window limit. For example, the Pro plan allows 45 requests per 5-hour window.
faq.a2: Window quota is counted by requests — each API call consumes one request. After exceeding the window limit, subsequent requests are billed at API rates (Lite plans are paused instead). You can view detailed request logs and billing in the console's Usage page.
faq.q3: What happens when I exceed the limit?
faq.a3: Lite plans are paused until the next 5-hour window resets. Pro and above plans continue working with overage billed at API rates. You can also purchase booster packs for temporary extra quota.
faq.q4: Can I upgrade or downgrade anytime?
faq.a4: Yes. Upgrades take effect immediately with prorated billing. Downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing period. You can manage this in the Subscription page of the console.
faq.a5: Booster packs are one-time quota top-ups not subject to the 5-hour window limit. After purchase, the quota is added directly to your account balance and is valid for 30 days. Available for Pro and above plans.

cta.description: Sign up free, get your API key, and start using AI tools in minutes.

api.title: API Documentation
api.description: Alaya Code provides OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible APIs for seamless integration with mainstream AI applications.
api.auth_desc: All API requests require an API Key in the HTTP Header for authentication:
api.auth_manage_prefix: You can create and manage API Keys in
api.chat_desc: Create chat completions with streaming and non-streaming responses. This is the most commonly used endpoint.
api.param_max_tokens_desc: Maximum number of tokens to generate
api.anthropic_desc: Anthropic Messages API compatible endpoint. For tools using the Anthropic SDK like Claude Code.
api.anthropic_note: Note: The Anthropic compatible API uses the x-api-key header instead of Authorization: Bearer.
api.rate_limits_desc: API requests are subject to rate limiting. Limits depend on your plan tier:

tools.title: Tool Integration Guide
tools.description: Alaya Code is compatible with OpenAI API and can directly integrate with mainstream AI coding tools. Here are configuration guides for each tool.
tools.cursor_desc: Cursor is a VSCode-based AI code editor that supports custom API endpoints.
tools.cursor_step1_desc: Open Cursor, click the gear icon in the top right, or use Ctrl+, (Windows/Linux) / Cmd+, (macOS).
tools.cursor_step2_desc: Find the "Models" option in settings and click "Add Model":
tools.cursor_step3_desc: After configuration, open a code file and use Ctrl+K or Ctrl+L to invoke AI features and verify the response.
tools.cursor_tip: You can add multiple models (e.g., kimi-2.5, qwen-3.5) and switch between them as needed.
tools.claude_desc: Claude Code is Anthropic's CLI AI assistant that supports custom API endpoints.
tools.claude_method1_desc: Add the following environment variables to your shell config file:
tools.claude_note: Claude Code uses the Anthropic API format (x-api-key). Alaya Code is fully compatible with this format.
tools.cline_desc: Cline is a popular AI coding extension for VSCode that supports custom OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
tools.cline_step1_desc: Search for "Cline" in the VSCode extension marketplace and install it.
tools.cline_step2_desc: Click the Cline icon in the VSCode sidebar, then click the gear icon to open settings.
tools.cline_step3_desc: Select "OpenAI Compatible" as the Provider and enter the following:
tools.cline_step4_desc: Type any question in the Cline panel to verify the response works correctly.
tools.opencode_desc: OpenCode is an open-source terminal AI coding assistant that supports custom API endpoints.
tools.tip1: Any tool supporting OpenAI API can connect to Alaya Code by modifying the Base URL.
tools.tip2: If a tool only supports the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable, simply set it to your Alaya Code key.
tools.tip3: Some tools may require manually specifying the model name. Make sure to use model IDs supported by Alaya Code.
tools.tip4: If you encounter connection issues, check whether the API Base URL needs to end with /v1 (some tools require it, some don't).

sdk.title: SDK Integration Guide
sdk.description: Alaya Code is compatible with the OpenAI SDK. You can use the official Python or Node.js SDK directly.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Frequently asked questions about Alaya Code.
faq.account_q1: What information is needed to register?
faq.account_a1: Registration only requires an email address and password. We also support GitHub and WeChat quick login. After registration, you'll receive free Lite plan quota to get started.
faq.account_q2: How much free quota do I get?
faq.account_a2: The Lite plan provides 10 free requests per 5-hour window. Enough for personal learning and light usage.
faq.account_q3: How do I get an API Key?
faq.account_a3: After logging in to the console, click "Create Key" on the "API Keys" page. We recommend creating separate keys for different projects or tools for better management and usage tracking.
faq.billing_a1: The 5-hour window is our billing cycle. The system resets your request count every 5 hours. Within a window, you can use the API normally as long as your requests stay under the window limit. For example, the Pro plan has 45 requests per 5-hour window.
faq.billing_q2: What happens when I exceed the limit?
faq.billing_a2: Lite plans are paused until the next 5-hour window resets. Pro and above plans continue working with overage billed at API rates.
faq.billing_a3: Booster packs are one-time quota top-ups not subject to the 5-hour window limit. After purchase, the quota is added to your account balance and is valid for 30 days. Available for Pro and above plans.
faq.billing_q4: Can I upgrade or downgrade anytime?
faq.billing_a4: Yes. Upgrades take effect immediately with prorated billing. Downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing period. Manage this in the "Subscription" page of the console.
faq.billing_a5: We currently support WeChat Pay and Alipay. Enterprise users can contact us for bank transfer arrangements.
faq.usage_a1: We currently support Kimi 2.5, Qwen 3.5, GLM 5, DeepSeek V3, Doubao Pro, and other major Chinese LLMs. We continuously update supported models — see the API docs for the latest list.
faq.usage_q2: Is the API fully compatible with OpenAI?
faq.usage_a2: Yes, Alaya Code is 100% compatible with the OpenAI API format, including Chat Completions, Embeddings, and Models endpoints. You can use the official OpenAI SDK directly — just change the base_url.
faq.usage_a3: Yes. Set stream: true in your request to enable SSE streaming output, fully compatible with OpenAI's streaming format.
faq.usage_a4: Our servers are deployed in China, connecting directly to LLM providers' APIs. Typical latency is 100-500ms (excluding model generation time). The experience is comparable to calling providers' APIs directly.
faq.usage_q5: How should I handle request failures?
faq.usage_a5: We recommend implementing exponential backoff retry. Common error codes include 429 (rate limit), 402 (insufficient quota), and 503 (service temporarily unavailable). See the Error Handling documentation for details.
faq.security_a1: No. We do not store any API request input or output content. We only log request metadata (time, model, token count) for billing and analytics.
faq.security_a2: API Keys are stored encrypted, and all transmission is HTTPS encrypted. You can disable or delete keys anytime in the console. We recommend regular key rotation and using separate keys for different projects.
faq.more_questions_suffix: . You can also submit a ticket in the console.

errors.title: Error Handling
errors.description: Learn about Alaya Code API error codes and recommended handling strategies.
errors.format_desc: When an API request fails, the response body contains error information in the following format:
errors.400_cause: Invalid JSON body or missing required fields (e.g., model, messages)
errors.400_solution: Check request body format and ensure all required fields are present
errors.401_cause: Invalid, expired, or missing API Key
errors.401_solution: Check the Authorization header format and API Key
errors.402_cause: Account balance exhausted or monthly spend cap reached
errors.402_solution: Upgrade plan or purchase a booster pack
errors.403_cause: Current plan doesn't support the requested model or feature
errors.403_solution: Upgrade to a plan that supports the model
errors.404_cause: Invalid API path or model name
errors.404_solution: Check the URL path and model name
errors.429_cause: Exceeded plan's RPM or TPM limit
errors.429_solution: Reduce request frequency or upgrade plan. Check Retry-After header
errors.500_solution: Retry the request. Contact support if it persists
errors.502_solution: Retry later or switch to another model
errors.retry_desc: For 429 and 5xx errors, implement exponential backoff retry:
errors.headers_desc: API responses include the following rate limit HTTP headers:
errors.header_retry: Suggested wait time in seconds for 429 errors
errors.troubleshoot_1: Verify API Key: Check in the console that the key is enabled. Watch for extra spaces when copying.
errors.troubleshoot_2: Check Base URL: Ensure it's https://api.alayanew.com/v1 (note the trailing /v1).
errors.troubleshoot_3: Check model name: Use GET /v1/models to see the list of currently available models.
errors.troubleshoot_4: Check quota: View current window quota usage on the Usage page in the console.
errors.troubleshoot_5: Review request logs: Check detailed API request records on the Logs page in the console.
